source_report: Propeller supply chain intelligence for the UAS/drone industry

propeller_manufacturers.name: HQProp
propeller_manufacturers.market_share_note: Largest propeller manufacturer — 44% of Forge DB. Durable T-mount series.

propeller_manufacturers.name: Gemfan (Ningbo Gemfan Hobby Co., Ltd)
propeller_manufacturers.market_share_note: Second-largest — 37%. ISO 9001:2015 certified. 51 design patents. OEM/ODM for professional UAV companies worldwide.
propeller_manufacturers.manufacturing: High-precision injection molding, CNC machining, dynamic balance testing

propeller_manufacturers.name: APC Propellers (Landing Products)
propeller_manufacturers.market_position: US-manufactured propellers. Primarily fixed-wing and larger multirotor. Glass-filled nylon. Used in enterprise/defense platforms.
propeller_manufacturers.note: APC is one of very few US-manufactured propeller brands. Strong in fixed-wing and larger drone market. Not competitive in FPV racing segment.

propeller_manufacturers.name: Lumenier (GetFPV)
propeller_manufacturers.ndaa_note: US brand. Propeller manufacturing likely Chinese CM (injection molding).

materials_supply_chain.summary: Propellers are injection-molded thermoplastics. No electronics, no firmware. The supply chain is raw plastic pellets → injection molding → dynamic balancing → packaging.

materials.name: Polycarbonate (PC)
materials.usage: Most common FPV propeller material. Flexible, crash-resistant.
materials.ndaa_status: Allied supply available (Covestro, SABIC). Chinese alternatives exist at lower cost.

materials.name: Glass fiber reinforced nylon (PA6-GF)
materials.usage: Stiffer propellers for higher efficiency. Less crash-resistant.

materials.name: Carbon fiber reinforced nylon
suppliers: Various — carbon fiber from Toray (Japan), nylon from BASF/DuPont
materials.ndaa_status: Allied supply chain for both components.

injection_molding.summary: Propeller injection molding is a commodity manufacturing process. The tooling (molds) requires precision engineering but is a one-time investment. Any injection molding shop can produce propellers.
injection_molding.chinese_dominance_reason: HQProp and Gemfan dominate because of aerodynamic design IP, mold tooling investment, and low labor cost — not because injection molding is unavailable elsewhere.
injection_molding.allied_capability: Injection molding is widely available in US/EU. APC (USA) demonstrates this. The barrier is mold design IP and cost, not capability.

ndaa_propeller_assessment.status: 96% Chinese but EASY to fix — no electronics, commodity manufacturing
ndaa_propeller_assessment.problem: HQProp (44%) and Gemfan (37%) control 81% of the market. Both Chinese. But propellers are injection-molded plastic with no electronics.
good_news: Raw materials (polycarbonate, nylon) available from allied sources (Covestro, BASF, DuPont)
Injection molding is commodity manufacturing available in any industrialized country
No electronics, firmware, or silicon dependency
Propeller design is relatively simple compared to other drone components
ndaa_propeller_assessment.barrier: Aerodynamic design IP — HQProp and Gemfan have 15+ years of blade profile optimization and 50+ patents. A new entrant would need significant R&D investment or licensing.
ndaa_propeller_assessment.realistic_path: For defense: APC (US-manufactured) for fixed-wing and larger drones. For FPV class: Lumenier could move injection molding to US. Or license Gemfan/HQProp designs for allied production.
ndaa_propeller_assessment.cost_premium: US injection molding adds ~2-3x cost. Propellers are consumable (pilots go through many sets), so cost matters more here than for durable components.
